description_long: This is a roughly hand-formed, round clay bucket or pot with a wide open mouth and a slightly irregular rim. The surface is matte and unglazed, showing a natural, earthy brown coloration with subtle variations and some visible surface imperfections, such as shallow scratches and faint cracks. The body is bulbous, tapering gently toward the base, and the interior appears unfinished but smooth. There are no visible handles, lids, or reinforcement bands, and the object is entirely made from a single material. Some faint, indistinct markings or impressions are present on one side, possibly from the forming process or incidental decoration. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian vessel, likely used for carrying or storing materials in an outdoor or rustic urban setting.
description: A hand-formed, round clay bucket or pot with a wide open mouth, matte brown surface, and no handles or lid.
description_view_0: This view shows the bucket's slightly uneven rim and some faint surface markings or impressions on the left side.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the smooth, continuous curve of the vessel and the absence of any handles or decorations.
description_view_2: This side reveals visible cracks and surface texture, emphasizing the handmade, rustic quality of the bucket.
description_view_3: This view displays a relatively featureless, smooth surface with subtle color variations and no visible markings.
